questions.question: A sinusoidal function has amplitude 3, period 4π, and passes through the origin with positive slope. Write the function in the form y = A sin(Bx + C).
questions.explanation: Amplitude A = 3. Period = 2π/B = 4π, so B = 1/2. Since the function passes through (0,0) with positive slope, C = 0. Thus y = 3sin(x/2).

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 35°, angle B = 80°, and side a = 12. Find side b using the Law of Sines. Round to one decimal place.
The graph of y = cos(2x) is shifted right by π/4 units. What is the equation of the new graph?
questions.explanation: Shifting right by π/4 replaces x with (x - π/4), giving y = cos(2(x - π/4)) = cos(2x - π/2). Using the cofunction identity, this equals -sin(2x).

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, sides x = 7, y = 10, and angle Z = 50°. Find side z using the Law of Cosines. Round to one decimal place.
The function y = tan(x) has a vertical asymptote at x = π/2. At what value of x does y = tan(3x) have its first vertical asymptote for x > 0?
questions.explanation: tan(3x) has vertical asymptotes when 3x = π/2 + nπ. For the first asymptote with x > 0, set 3x = π/2, so x = π/6.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, all three sides are known: p = 8, q = 6, r = 7. Use the Law of Cosines to find angle Q. Round to the nearest degree.
questions.explanation: By Law of Cosines: q² = p² + r² - 2pr·cos(Q). So 36 = 64 + 49 - 2(8)(7)cos(Q), giving 36 = 113 - 112cos(Q). Thus cos(Q) = 77/112 ≈ 0.688, and Q ≈ arccos(0.688) ≈ 49°.
